behavior_summary: Provides the short description for the rope object when the player inspects it.
When the U8P_ROPE::look process runs it emits a single bark/text line: "rope".
After displaying the text the process yields/ends (raw node 'suspend' present — inferred termination behavior).
No state changes, global flags, menus, branching, or external calls are performed.
notable_control_flow: Linear, single-step flow: enter U8P_ROPE::look -> bark "rope" -> suspend/terminate the U8P_ROPE::look process.
No conditional branches or loops are present.
open_questions: Does the raw 'suspend' node correspond to engine-level process termination or just a brief yield that allows other processes to run? (inferred, not explicit)
Is this object referenced by other scripts (use/interaction) elsewhere in the game, or is it purely decorative/examinable?

main_function.name: U8P_ROPE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_ROPE::look' (examine) action for the rope by showing a short descriptive line.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses the 'U8P_ROPE::look' (examine) action on the rope object.
Classified as a U8P_ROPE::look-type process (processType 0x0208) — engine triggers on object inspection.

function_map.name: U8P_ROPE::look
function_map.role: U8P_ROPE::look handler (gives short description when examined)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the short description for the rope object when the player inspects it.
